task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into the system using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the user logged into the medical system, typically indicated by a dashboard, welcome message, or user profile icon showing 'admin'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the medical record for Maria Maggio (DOB: 1973-05-01).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a patient chart or dashboard with the name 'Maria Maggio' and DOB '1973-05-01' clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected or typed 'Penicillin' as the drug allergen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an allergy entry form with 'Penicillin' populated in the allergen or drug name field.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent documented 'hives' as the reaction to the allergen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'hives' entered or selected in the reaction field of the allergy form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent set the severity to 'moderate' and the status to 'active'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'moderate' selected in the severity dropdown/field and 'active' selected in the status dropdown/field.

task_completion.description: Verify the allergy was successfully saved to the patient's record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the patient's active allergy list displaying Penicillin with the specified reaction (hives) and severity (moderate).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual medical software GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard web browser or application UI elements (buttons, forms, dropdowns) being used, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just type the expected output into a text editor or notepad.
integrity.visual_evidence: The allergy information is entered into a structured medical record interface, not a plain text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the final allergy list is a genuine part of the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final confirmation screen matches the styling and layout of the medical application, showing a persistent record rather than a temporary text overlay.
